cycle_history.best_hook: I've been deep in the veDEX rabbit hole for weeks, and MarbMarket's fair launch on MegaETH feels different. No presale. No VCs. Just pure community power. A veDEX lets you lock MARB tokens to gain voting power. Longer locks = more influence + more rewards. Weekly votes decide which LP pools get MARB emissions. Projects can "bribe" for votes. It's wild. The incentive loop is beautiful: MARB lockers direct emissions, LPs get rewards, protocols pay bribes, incentives flow back to lockers. @RallyOnChain is tracking this closely. x.com/Marb_market is where you need to be. Not sure how the ve(3,3) model will play out on MegaETH yet. Thoughts?

cycle_history.best_hook: Spent the last few days digging into the vote-escrow model and I think it fixes the biggest problem in liquidity mining. Instead of emissions just going to the highest APY farm, token lockers actually vote on where the rewards go. Projects then offer bribes to capture those votes. It creates a circular economy where governance power directly translates to yield. MarbMarket is bringing this exact mechanic to MegaETH as the first veDEX there. No presale and no VC money either. The whole thing relies on users locking MARB to get veTokens, which lets them direct emissions to specific LP pools. @RallyOnChain caught my attention with this one. I'm curious to see if the community can actually sustain the bribe markets without external capital. Check the launch details at x.com/Marb_market

You lock your token to get voting power, longer lock means more influence. Then you vote on where the liquidity rewards go each week. It turns LPs into mercenaries and voters into generals. MarbMarket is bringing this exact ve(3,3) mechanic to MegaETH without a presale or VC bag. That is rare. Protocols can bribe voters to direct emissions to their pools, so the cycle actually feeds back to the people who commit capital. I think the fair launch aspect here might be the most important part. @RallyOnChain has been building something interesting here. Check out the mechanics at x.com/Marb_market

I think this is a pretty big deal because it is the first veDEX on the chain and there is zero presale or VC involvement. Just a fair launch with LP farming and voting live from day one. Then you vote on where the weekly liquidity mining rewards go. Protocols bribe you for those votes to incentivize liquidity for their specific pairs. It turns governance into a marketplace. MarbMarket is bringing this exact model to MegaETH as the first veDEX there. No presale and no VC allocation. Just a fair launch with LP farming, voting, and bribes available from day one. winning_hooks.hook: I've been diving into veDEX mechanics lately and MarbMarket's approach to MegaETH feels different. Most DEXs just throw tokens at liquidity. MarbMarket flips this. You lock MARB to get veTokens, which control weekly emissions. Longer locks mean more influence. It's not just farming. It's governance.

I've been deep in the veDEX rabbit hole for weeks, and MarbMarket's fair launch on MegaETH feels different. No presale. No VCs. Just pure community power. A veDEX lets you lock MARB tokens to gain voting power. Longer locks = more influence + more rewards. Weekly votes decide which LP pools get MARB emissions. Projects can "bribe" for votes. It's wild. The incentive loop is beautiful: MARB lockers direct emissions, LPs get rewards, protocols pay bribes, incentives flow back to lockers. @RallyOnChain is tracking this closely. x.com/Marb_market is where you need to be. Not sure how the ve(3,3) model will play out on MegaETH yet. Thoughts?

Spent the last few days digging into the vote-escrow model and I think it fixes the biggest problem in liquidity mining. Instead of emissions just going to the highest APY farm, token lockers actually vote on where the rewards go. Projects then offer bribes to capture those votes. It creates a circular economy where governance power directly translates to yield. MarbMarket is bringing this exact mechanic to MegaETH as the first veDEX there. No presale and no VC money either. The whole thing relies on users locking MARB to get veTokens, which lets them direct emissions to specific LP pools. @RallyOnChain caught my attention with this one. I'm curious to see if the community can actually sustain the bribe markets without external capital. Check the launch details at x.com/Marb_market

Then you vote on where the liquidity rewards go each week. It turns LPs into mercenaries and voters into generals. MarbMarket is bringing this exact ve(3,3) mechanic to MegaETH without a presale or VC bag. That is rare. Protocols can bribe voters to direct emissions to their pools, so the cycle actually feeds back to the people who commit capital. I think the fair launch aspect here might be the most important part. @RallyOnChain has been building something interesting here. Check out the mechanics at x.com/Marb_market
